source_idea.raw_idea: We need to develop an executor. It takes an input task description, where the basic task definition contains work environment, permissions, and skill configuration. Through an already formed ClaudeTmuxAdapter component, it calls Claude CLI.

scope.mvp_boundary: Executor accepts a task description containing work environment, permissions, and skill configuration, then uses ClaudeTmuxAdapter to call Claude CLI. CLI shape, delivery contract, and completion authority are not yet confirmed.

payload.one_line_summary: An executor that runs task descriptions through Claude CLI by using ClaudeTmuxAdapter.
target_users: command-line users who need local agent-assisted task execution
payload.problem_statement: Users need a command-driven executor that can translate a structured task description into Claude CLI execution.
payload.value_proposition: Reuse the existing ClaudeTmuxAdapter instead of rebuilding Claude session control.

payload.statement: Build the executor capability around task descriptions and ClaudeTmuxAdapter-based Claude CLI invocation.

payload.title: Create executor
payload.description: Develop an executor that runs work from an input task description.
payload.user_value: A user can provide a task description and have the executor start the work.

payload.title: Parse task definition context
payload.description: The task description includes a basic task definition with work environment, permissions, and skill configuration.
payload.user_value: Claude execution receives the intended context and constraints.

payload.title: Use ClaudeTmuxAdapter
payload.description: The executor calls Claude CLI through an already formed ClaudeTmuxAdapter component.
payload.user_value: The executor reuses the existing Claude CLI session adapter.

payload.title: Confirm executor invocation boundary
payload.description: The executor's user-facing invocation method is a local CLI command such as `executor run <task-file>`.
payload.user_value: The first implementation has a stable command and input contract.

payload.title: Confirm delivery and completion contract
payload.description: The executor produces a structured JSON result plus execution log, and task completion is judged by a separate agent or reviewer against task expectations.
payload.user_value: The executor can determine when a task is complete without inventing success criteria.

payload.criterion: The executor can start a run from a provided task description after the invocation boundary is confirmed.
The task definition exposes work environment, permissions, and skill configuration to the executor.
Claude CLI invocation is routed through ClaudeTmuxAdapter instead of direct Claude process control.
The MVP executor exposes a local CLI boundary such as `executor run <task-file>`.
The executor emits a structured JSON result plus execution log, and completion is accepted by a separate agent or reviewer.

payload.description: Load task description, extract context, call ClaudeTmuxAdapter to invoke Claude CLI, then await confirmed delivery and completion rules before declaring readiness.
Task definition data includes work environment, permissions, and skill configuration; delivery and done fields are currently unresolved.
Executor orchestration module.
ClaudeTmuxAdapter integration boundary.
Executor must use ClaudeTmuxAdapter to call Claude CLI.
Task description contains work environment, permissions, and skill configuration.
Input is a task description containing work environment, permissions, and skill configuration.

Run one local CLI invocation, load the task file, pass task context to Claude through ClaudeTmuxAdapter, collect execution evidence, and hand the result to a separate agent or reviewer for acceptance.
Verify local CLI invocation, task context parsing, ClaudeTmuxAdapter routing, structured JSON result output, execution log output, and separate agent or reviewer acceptance.
MVP output is a structured JSON result plus execution log.
Stop when task file input is unavailable, task context is invalid, ClaudeTmuxAdapter cannot invoke Claude CLI, or separate agent/reviewer acceptance fails.
Done means the executor has completed the local CLI run, emitted structured JSON and logs, and received completion acceptance from a separate agent or reviewer.
Executor readiness could be overclaimed if CLI shape, delivery output, or completion authority are invented.
Do not render ready PRDs or HLD until unresolved execution and delivery decisions are confirmed.
ClaudeTmuxAdapter is available and already formed, as stated by the user.

payload.question: Which executor invocation boundary should Stage 1 use?
payload.impact: Human PRD and Agent PRD cannot be review-ready without a stable user-facing entry point.
options: A. Local CLI only, for example `executor run <task-file>`.
B. Local CLI plus library/API entry point.
C. Keep blocked until the invocation boundary is specified.

payload.question: Which delivery output should the executor produce in the MVP?
payload.impact: Agent PRD cannot be execution-ready without output deliverables.
options: A. Final textual result plus execution log.
B. Structured JSON result plus execution log.
C. Keep blocked until delivery output is specified.

payload.question: Who is authoritative for deciding task completion?
payload.impact: Done criteria and verification cannot be made ready without this decision.
options: B. A separate agent or reviewer validates completion against task expectations.
C. Keep blocked until completion authority is specified.

payload.content: We need to develop an executor. It takes an input task description, where the basic task definition contains work environment, permissions, and skill configuration. Through an already formed ClaudeTmuxAdapter component, it calls Claude CLI.
payload.label: Original user idea

payload.content: User selected Q-001=A: local CLI only, for example `executor run <task-file>`; Q-002=B: structured JSON result plus execution log; Q-003=B: a separate agent or reviewer validates completion against task expectations.
payload.label: Closed-choice Stage 1 confirmation

payload.name: Stage 1 clarification gate
payload.blocking_condition: Invocation boundary, delivery output, and completion authority are unresolved.

gate_report.message: Stage 1 closed-choice questions were resolved by user confirmation.

next_actions: Render Human PRD and Agent PRD from this validated Stage 1 contract in Stage 2.

requirement_table.problem: A user needs an executor that can run work from a task description.
requirement_table.user_value: The user can submit task intent and have executor drive Claude CLI execution.
requirement_table.mvp_scope: Develop the executor capability around task descriptions.
requirement_table.acceptance_summary: Executor can start from a provided task description after invocation boundary is confirmed.

requirement_table.problem: Claude execution needs the task's environment, permissions, and skill context.
requirement_table.user_value: The executor can pass the intended constraints and available skills into the execution flow.
requirement_table.mvp_scope: Task definition includes work environment, permissions, and skill configuration.
requirement_table.acceptance_summary: These fields are represented in the executor input contract.

requirement_table.problem: The executor needs a reliable way to call Claude CLI.
requirement_table.user_value: The executor can reuse the already formed ClaudeTmuxAdapter component.
requirement_table.mvp_scope: Route Claude CLI invocation through ClaudeTmuxAdapter.
requirement_table.acceptance_summary: Executor does not directly control Claude CLI when ClaudeTmuxAdapter is the stated component.

requirement_table.problem: The executor invocation boundary must be stable for MVP implementation.
requirement_table.user_value: A stable entry point prevents implementation drift.
requirement_table.mvp_scope: Use local CLI only, for example `executor run <task-file>`.
requirement_table.acceptance_summary: The MVP exposes a local CLI command and does not include a library/API entry point.

requirement_table.problem: Delivery output and completion authority must be explicit before execution readiness.
requirement_table.user_value: The executor can avoid inventing done criteria.
requirement_table.mvp_scope: Emit structured JSON result plus execution log; use a separate agent or reviewer for completion acceptance.
requirement_table.acceptance_summary: The MVP produces JSON/log output and accepts completion only after separate agent or reviewer validation.

interaction_decision.reason: The user resolved invocation boundary, delivery output, and completion authority through closed-choice confirmation.
